pi_items.source: Task description (task.json) and verification script (verifier.py) both require creation of unit group with this exact name
Task description (task.json) and verification script (verifier.py) both require creation of product flow with this exact name
Task description (task.json) and verification script (verifier.py) both require creation of process with this exact name
Task description specifies Bottle as reference unit (Conversion factor: 1.0). Verified as correct reference unit definition in beverage packaging.

Web search confirms 24 bottles per case is standard industry practice for beverage packaging (https://theflavorexperts.com/how-many-bottles-are-in-a-case/, https://www.corecans.com/case-trays/standard-24-can-case-trays-3). Task description specifies 24.0 conversion factor.

Web search confirms 1920 bottles per pallet is a standard beverage industry configuration (80 cases × 24 bottles). Multiple sources confirm this (https://www.amazon.com/Waterpallets-MM-16-9-Oz-delivered/dp/B0DV89CZ49, https://prefaceplast.com/how-many-cases-of-beer-on-a-pallet). Task description specifies 1920.0 conversion factor.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires users to create synthetic data within openLCA: a Unit Group named 'Beverage Packaging' with three units (Bottle as 1.0 reference unit, Case at 24.0, and Pallet at 1920.0), a Product Flow named 'Cola 500mL' using this unit group, and a Process named 'Cola Palletizing' with 1.0 Pallet output. All conversion factors match verified industry standards for beverage packaging: 24 bottles per case is standard practice, and 1920 bottles per pallet (80 cases × 24 bottles) is a confirmed real-world configuration used in beverage distribution.
